forum_posts.thread: Apply MCDP 4 principles to a 30-day MEU sustainment
forum_posts.body: Applying responsiveness, the MEU's CLB needs the LCE to push 5-day pulses rather than waiting for pull from the BLT. Per MCDP 4 Ch 2 the responsiveness principle implies forward-positioned stocks.

forum_posts.thread: Class V planning factors — discussion
forum_posts.body: MCWP 4-11 Ch 3 has the consumption tables. I used the offensive operations baseline.

forum_posts.thread: Where does sustainment estimate fit in MCPP?
forum_posts.body: It feeds COA wargaming in step 4 of MCPP. The logistic estimate has to be live so the wargame doesn't pick a COA we cannot sustain.

forum_posts.thread: Critique: simplicity vs flexibility in MCDP 4
forum_posts.body: Simplicity argues for fewer SKU lines and standardized loadouts. Flexibility argues for tailored task-organized packages. Tension is real and the doctrine glosses it. I'd weight simplicity at the BLT level and flexibility at the LCE level.

forum_posts.thread: RSOI throughput — calculation walkthrough
forum_posts.body: Used MCRP 4-11.1F App C. My APOD throughput calc came out to 1,200 STON/day given two C-17 spots and a 22-hr ops day.

forum_posts.thread: How does GCSS-MC handle cross-leveling?
forum_posts.body: Through the requisition module I think. We covered this in PMOS school but I have to look it up again.

forum_posts.thread: Logistics rebellion — when does the tail wag the dog?
forum_posts.body: Sustainment becomes operational reach. If the planner doesn't model culmination per MCRP 3-40D para 5, the COA will overrun its DOS and the tail dictates the new mission.

forum_posts.thread: MEU sustainment estimate — peer review
forum_posts.body: Peer reviewed S04's draft. Suggested rebuilding Class III(B) on JP-8 consumption per fuel-burner type, not per platform-day.

forum_posts.thread: What's the worst sustainment failure you've seen written up?
forum_posts.body: The Fallujah sustainment AAR — Class IX backlog hurt us at the company-level.

forum_posts.thread: MCDP 4 principle of survivability — apply to drone-saturated environment
forum_posts.body: Survivability now demands distributed sustainment — fewer big nodes, more small nodes. Not in the doctrine yet.

submission_history.assignment: Squad in the Defense — Written Check

forum_posts.thread: Squad in the defense — sectors of fire
forum_posts.body: Combining MCRP 3-10A.4 Ch 5 with MCWP 3-01 Ch 5: the squad's sector should overlap with the adjacent squad's by 50m to deny the enemy a seam, but the leader has to publish the seam owner explicitly or both squads will assume the other has it.

forum_posts.thread: Mission-type orders in DDIL — what really works?
forum_posts.body: MCDP 6 Ch 3 says mission-type orders. In DDIL it's not enough — you need pre-briefed contingencies because the orient step in OODA stalls when comms degrade. The doctrine is right in spirit but soft on the EW reality.

forum_posts.thread: Casualty evac — squad-level decision authority
forum_posts.body: The doctrine pushes CASEVAC launch to platoon. I'd push it to squad. The few minutes saved are decisive. That's a doctrine + policy conversation.

forum_posts.thread: Reverse-slope defense — when?
forum_posts.body: When the enemy has overhead ISR or armor, reverse slope removes their direct observation. MCWP 3-01 covers this but the modern ISR threat makes it more important than the doctrine implies.

forum_posts.thread: Squad fire and movement — the basics
forum_posts.body: Per MCRP 3-10A.4: bounding overwatch with team-level fire support. We rehearsed last week and it worked.

forum_posts.thread: Mentorship in MCRP 6-11D — what stuck?
forum_posts.body: The five horizontal themes — fidelity, fortitude, initiative, perseverance, integrity. I keep them on my notebook cover.

forum_posts.thread: Tactical patience — when do you wait vs press?
forum_posts.body: Doctrine doesn't give you a clean answer. My rule: press when you have initiative and intel. Wait when you have neither. Doctrine implies always press; that's wrong against a peer.

forum_posts.thread: Decision games — getting the most out of them
forum_posts.body: Most TDGs ask 'what would you do?' The harder ones ask 'what would you do at T+15s, T+45s, T+2m?' Doctrine doesn't teach you to think on multiple time horizons.

forum_posts.thread: Heat casualty mitigation — leader actions
forum_posts.body: Per the heat cat SOPs: water plan briefed, work-rest cycles enforced, a buddy-check at the top of every hour. We had a near-miss last summer because I missed a buddy check window.

forum_posts.thread: How do you brief commander's intent for a junior?
forum_posts.body: Strip it to: end state, two key tasks, one thing you absolutely don't want. That's it. Anything more and the junior won't carry it forward under stress.

forum_posts.thread: PMCS — what's the difference between scheduled and corrective?
forum_posts.body: Scheduled is on a calendar. Corrective is when something breaks. From MCO 4790.25.

forum_posts.thread: GCSS-MC requisition flow — walk through
forum_posts.body: You log in, you make the requisition, it routes. I'm still learning the screens.

forum_posts.thread: Class IX demand forecast — how do you do it?
forum_posts.body: Look at past consumption. Project forward. I think there's a tool in GCSS for this.

forum_posts.thread: Why is PMCS important?
forum_posts.body: It catches problems before they become deadlines. Keeps equipment ready.

forum_posts.thread: Recovery operations — squad responsibilities
forum_posts.body: Per MCWP 4-11.4 — we have to recover or evac. Cross-level if we can.

forum_posts.thread: What's the RREL?
forum_posts.body: Readiness reportable equipment list. From MCO 4790.25 Ch 4.

forum_posts.thread: Echelons of maintenance — confused on field vs sustainment
forum_posts.body: Field is at the operating unit. Sustainment is higher. I keep forgetting which is which.

forum_posts.thread: Class V planning — never done it before
forum_posts.body: I haven't done a Class V plan. Asking for help. Anyone done one for a rifle company?

forum_posts.thread: GCSS-MC outage — what do we do?
forum_posts.body: I think we go to manual requisitions. The SOP is in the binder somewhere. I have to find it.

forum_posts.thread: Why did my PMCS Quiz score so low?
forum_posts.body: I missed the questions on calendar-based intervals. Going to re-read MCO 4790.25 Ch 2 this week.
